jokes.text: Anna berating Shlomo in Yiddish then immediately switching to polite 'I'm sorry. Have a good day' to Larry
jokes.setup: Anna furiously yelling at her husband in Yiddish about being incompetent
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to sweet customer service voice for Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment and cultural specificity, great contrast between private rage and public politeness

jokes.text: I bet you make her very happy
jokes.setup: Anna complimenting Larry on making his wife happy
jokes.punchline: Sets up Larry's response about his wife being miserable
jokes.explanation: Solid setup that works through dramatic irony - we know what's coming

jokes.text: No, she's the most miserable woman in the world, believe me
jokes.setup: Anna's assumption that Larry makes his wife happy
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact correction that she's actually miserable
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David self-awareness and brutal honesty delivered with perfect deadpan timing

jokes.text: I run away from hoodlums... it keeps you in shape
jokes.setup: Anna asking how Larry stays fit
jokes.punchline: Larry's unconventional fitness routine of fleeing from criminals
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's neurotic worldview - turning paranoia into practical fitness advice

jokes.text: He got hit by a bike messenger
jokes.setup: Rabbi explaining his brother-in-law died on 9/11, implying he was in the towers
jokes.punchline: Revealing he died from a completely unrelated bike accident uptown
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that subverts expectations about 9/11 tragedy in a darkly funny way

jokes.text: Larry confessing to the rabbi about his wife's 10th anniversary 'gift' of permission to cheat
jokes.setup: Larry asking for private counsel from the rabbi
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate nature of what he's confessing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry crossing social boundaries by discussing sex with a religious figure

jokes.text: Get the hell outta here!
jokes.setup: Rabbi citing biblical precedent for extramarital relations
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked disbelief at this revelation
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Larry's surprise at finding biblical support

jokes.text: I'm a bad person anyway, but I'm not... am I an extra bad person?
jokes.setup: Larry seeking moral guidance about adultery
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact acceptance that he's already bad, just wondering about degrees
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David self-awareness and twisted moral reasoning delivered with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Do survivors like seeing each other? Do they like to talk about old times or does the sight of another survivor depress them?
jokes.setup: Larry wondering whether to invite another Holocaust survivor to dinner
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate phrasing treating Holocaust survivors like a social experiment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's tendency to overthink social situations in tone-deaf ways

jokes.text: this is continuing into the afterlife?
jokes.setup: Cheryl reading vows about loving through eternity
jokes.punchline: Larry's horror at discovering marriage extends beyond death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant premise - Larry treating eternal love as a contractual obligation he didn't understand

jokes.text: I thought I'd be single, I guess
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking about Larry's problem with eternal marriage
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual admission he planned to be single in the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Larry's selfishness delivered with devastating understatement

jokes.text: He's the survivor. From the... from the television show. 'Survivor.'
jokes.setup: Larry expecting a Holocaust survivor at dinner
jokes.punchline: The 'survivor' is from the reality TV show
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that sets up the entire episode's central conflict

jokes.text: The entire argument between Colby and Solly comparing Survivor to the Holocaust
jokes.setup: Two 'survivors' meeting and trying to relate to each other
jokes.punchline: The absurd comparison of reality TV hardship to actual Holocaust suffering
jokes.explanation: The episode's centerpiece - brilliantly uncomfortable escalation between two completely different types of 'survival'

jokes.text: The escalating survivor argument: Colby talks about deadly snakes and no snacks, Solly counters with concentration camps and no food for weeks
jokes.setup: Reality show contestant describing hardships
jokes.punchline: Holocaust survivor's increasingly outraged responses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing the absurdity of comparing reality TV to genocide. Each exchange gets more outrageous.

jokes.text: We didn't have one. We had 12 people at a time would go and shit... Well I'm sure you had toilet paper. We had newspaper.
jokes.setup: Colby complaining about lack of bathroom facilities on Survivor
jokes.punchline: Solly's increasingly graphic descriptions of Holocaust conditions
jokes.explanation: Peak escalation in the survivor comparison - increasingly graphic details create maximum discomfort

jokes.text: Mosquito?! You see this glass eye?!
jokes.setup: Colby complaining about mosquitoes on Survivor
jokes.punchline: Solly revealing his glass eye as evidence of real suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation using a shocking visual reveal to trump TV show complaints

jokes.text: It was called the Holocaust!
jokes.setup: Colby referring to Survivor as 'our show'
jokes.punchline: Solly treating the Holocaust as a competing television program
jokes.explanation: Brilliant climax to the escalation - reducing historical tragedy to TV show competition

jokes.text: Somebody get a sponge
jokes.setup: Chaos from the survivor argument, wine spilled
jokes.punchline: Larry making the same insensitive demand that will cause more problems
jokes.explanation: Establishes what will become a key running gag - Larry's oblivious insensitivity

jokes.text: What? I just told her to get a sponge, that's all
jokes.setup: Everyone looking at Larry after his sponge comment
jokes.punchline: Larry's complete obliviousness to why this might be inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David obliviousness - he genuinely doesn't understand the problem

jokes.text: Oh, okay. No wonder why
jokes.setup: Larry's suit missing from dry cleaning when husband working instead of wife
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual sexism implying women are more competent
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry casual prejudice delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: You've been stalking me, haven't you, Larry?
jokes.setup: Larry explaining how he found her house by seeing the van
jokes.punchline: Anna playfully interpreting his explanation as romantic pursuit
jokes.explanation: Sets up the flirtation that leads to Larry's opportunity

jokes.text: I can take you piggyback
jokes.setup: Anna saying she can't drive on the Sabbath but they could walk
jokes.punchline: Larry's ridiculous solution to avoid making her walk
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Larry solution showing his awkward attempts at chivalry

jokes.text: 10 long years
jokes.setup: Larry mentioning he's been married 10 years
jokes.punchline: Anna's weary emphasis on 'long' revealing her own marital dissatisfaction
jokes.explanation: Nice parallel between Larry and Anna's marital situations

jokes.text: The Hassids, they have sex through a sheet
jokes.setup: Larry saying he set something up with the Hasidic woman
jokes.punchline: Jeff explaining the Orthodox Jewish sexual practice
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference joke that relies on audience knowledge but sets up the sheet subplot

jokes.text: You should spring for a satin sheet. You'd be sliding all over
jokes.setup: Discussion of what kind of sheet Larry should bring
jokes.punchline: Jeff's practical consideration of sheet material for sexual logistics
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy imagining the practical problems of satin sheets

jokes.text: Because of your sponge comment
jokes.setup: Cheryl's mother refusing to come to the ceremony
jokes.punchline: The sponge incident has escalated into family conflict
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Larry's thoughtless comments have consequences

jokes.text: it just brought up a lot of stuff about the Holocaust, and it's kind of in my DNA and I was very flummoxed by it
jokes.setup: Larry trying to apologize for the sponge comment
jokes.punchline: His bizarre explanation blaming Holocaust DNA for his rudeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David pseudo-intellectual excuse making - using trauma to justify bad behavior

jokes.text: the light from your glass eye, it's reflecting on me
jokes.setup: Solly accusing Larry of mimicking him in the car
jokes.punchline: Larry's explanation that he's reacting to light reflection from the glass eye
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier reveal with added visual comedy element

jokes.text: Solly's glass eye reflection causing Larry to make gestures that look like mockery: 'He's mimicking me.' 'You-you were making fun of me!'
jokes.setup: In the limo, Solly's glass eye catches light
jokes.punchline: Larry's innocent reaction to glare looks like deliberate mockery
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy setup that creates maximum misunderstanding from innocent actions.

jokes.text: Somebody get a sponge
jokes.setup: Solly gets out of the car, leaving everyone confused
jokes.punchline: Larry repeating the exact same insensitive demand
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Larry has learned nothing from earlier consequences

jokes.text: That's when everybody yells a 'matza toff'? No no, it's mazel tov
jokes.setup: Rabbi explaining the glass-breaking ceremony
jokes.punchline: Larry mispronouncing 'mazel tov' as 'matza toff'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation joke that fits Larry's character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Reach into Larry's pocket for an unwrapped mint? Sure
jokes.setup: Larry's awkward mint-offering scenario
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's sarcastic acceptance of the inappropriate request
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Cheryl's long-suffering patience with Larry's weirdness

jokes.text: Larry's 'Let's roll' comment triggering the Rabbi because his brother-in-law died on 9/11: 'How dare you say something like that!'
jokes.setup: Larry innocently saying 'let's roll' to start ceremony
jokes.punchline: Rabbi connecting it to 9/11 Flight 93 and his brother-in-law's death
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that creates maximum awkwardness from innocent phrase. The escalation is perfect.

jokes.text: I didn't know that an uptown death on West 57th Street was part of the tragedy
jokes.setup: Larry defending his 'Let's roll' comment
jokes.punchline: His completely tone-deaf technical argument about what counts as 9/11-related
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David response - doubling down with technical correctness while missing the emotional point

jokes.text: Things have been good. It's a very good... relationship... 10 years. It's pretty good. It's pretty pretty... pretty pretty good
jokes.setup: Larry attempting to deliver heartfelt wedding vows
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to express genuine emotion, falling back on 'pretty good'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - his signature 'pretty good' becomes the height of romantic expression

jokes.text: I am your devoted servant... well I don't know about servant, but... I'm not a servant
jokes.setup: Larry trying to make romantic promises
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracking and clarifying the limits of his commitment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of romantic language - Larry can't help but add disclaimers to his vows

jokes.text: after death who knows what happens? Even you... you don't know
jokes.setup: Larry reluctantly promising to love through eternity
jokes.punchline: Challenging the rabbi's religious authority about the afterlife
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Larry still hasn't accepted the eternity concept, even during vows

jokes.text: Somebody get a sponge
jokes.setup: Larry cuts his hand on the glass, blood everywhere
jokes.punchline: Third repetition of the sponge demand, now for his own blood
jokes.explanation: Perfect running gag culmination - the phrase that caused so much trouble now applies to Larry's own predicament

jokes.text: It's about fucking time, Larry. My God, I've been waiting all day
jokes.setup: Larry arriving at Anna's for their arranged encounter
jokes.punchline: Anna's hilariously impatient greeting for what's supposed to be a romantic liaison
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between romantic expectations and mundane impatience

jokes.text: Fuck or suit?
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to discuss dry cleaning during foreplay
jokes.punchline: Anna's blunt ultimatum reducing the choice to its essence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of Larry's confused priorities delivered with perfect bluntness

jokes.text: What kind of fucking idiot are you?
jokes.setup: Larry bringing a sheet with a hole cut in it
jokes.punchline: Anna's incredulous reaction to Larry believing the Orthodox sex myth
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Larry fell for the sheet myth completely

jokes.text: Hey, we survived
jokes.setup: Earthquake interrupting Larry and Anna
jokes.punchline: Anna's comment about 'surviving' echoing the episode's survivor theme
jokes.explanation: Nice thematic callback that ties the episode's survival theme to the final scene
